title: Big Boost for E-commerce Exports! CBIC Rolls Out Key Courier Trade Reforms
content:
# Big Boost for E-commerce Exports! CBIC Rolls Out Key Courier Trade Reforms

Ek important update hai! Government ne e-commerce exports aur broader courier-based trade ko strengthen aur streamline karne ke liye comprehensive reforms announce kiye hain, effective 1st April 2026. These measures are designed to enhance ease of doing business and boost India's global export competitiveness, especially for MSMEs, artisans, and start-ups.

Here's a quick lowdown on the key changes:

*   **No More ₹10 Lakh Value Cap on Courier Exports:** The existing value limit of ₹10 lakh per consignment for commercial exports through courier mode has been completely removed. This means e-commerce exporters now have greater flexibility in shipment value and can utilize the courier mode for higher-value consignments, eliminating the need to shift them to conventional air or sea cargo due to value restrictions.
*   **Return to Origin (RTO) for Uncleared Imports:** A legally backed Return to Origin (RTO) mechanism has been introduced for imported goods that remain uncleared or unclaimed for more than 15 days at International Courier Terminals. This simplified procedure, applicable if goods are not prohibited, restricted, or under enforcement hold, aims to ease congestion and improve logistics efficiency.
*   **Simplified Re-import of Returned/Rejected Goods:** The procedure for re-import of returned or rejected goods, including those from e-commerce exports, has been simplified. A risk-based approach will now be adopted instead of consignment-wise verification, along with a dedicated return module in the Express Cargo Clearance System for smoother processing.

These reforms were operationalized through the following:

*   Notification No. 33/2026-Customs (N.T.) dated March 31, 2026, amending the Courier Imports and Exports (Electronic Declaration and Processing) Regulations 2010.
*   Notification No. 34/2026-Customs (N.T.) dated March 31, 2026, amending the Courier Imports and Exports (Clearance) Regulations, 1998.
*   Circular No. 17/2026-Customs dated March 31, 2026, explaining the amendments and detailing the operational modalities.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.cbic.gov.in/resources//htdocs-cbec/customs/cs-circulars/cs-circulars-2026/circular-no-17-2026-customs.pdf)

*Published on: 2 April 2026*
